questions.question: Why can a polynomial of degree n not have more than n zeroes?
questions.answer: Because the maximum number of x-axis intersections equals the degree.

questions.question: Why is the degree of the zero polynomial not defined?
questions.answer: Because it has no non-zero term to determine the highest power.

questions.question: Why does a linear polynomial intersect the x-axis at only one point?
questions.answer: Because it has degree one and forms a straight line.

questions.question: Why does a quadratic polynomial have at most two zeroes?
questions.answer: Because its graph is a parabola of degree two.

questions.question: Why does a constant polynomial generally have no zeroes?
questions.answer: Because its graph is a horizontal line not intersecting the x-axis.

questions.question: Why is p(0) equal to the constant term?
questions.answer: Because all terms containing x become zero when x = 0.

questions.question: Why does the graph of a polynomial help find zeroes?
questions.answer: Because zeroes correspond to x-axis intersections.

questions.question: Why can a polynomial have fewer zeroes than its degree?
questions.answer: Because some roots may be imaginary or repeated.

questions.question: Why is ax² + bx not a linear polynomial?
questions.answer: Because the highest power of x is 2.

questions.question: Why does the graph of a constant polynomial never slope?
questions.answer: Because the value of the polynomial remains the same for all x.

questions.question: Why can a polynomial have repeated zeroes?
questions.answer: Because the graph may touch the x-axis without cutting it.

questions.question: Why does the degree depend only on the highest power?
questions.answer: Because the highest power determines the end behavior of the graph.

questions.question: Why is graphical method not exact?
questions.answer: Because graph reading depends on scale and approximation.

questions.question: Why is x² − 4 symmetric about the y-axis?
questions.answer: Because it contains only even powers of x.

questions.question: Why is the graph of a cubic polynomial not symmetric?
questions.answer: Because it contains odd powers of x.

questions.question: Why does substituting a zero make the polynomial value zero?
questions.answer: Because zeroes are defined as values that satisfy p(x) = 0.

questions.question: Why is degree important in studying polynomials?
questions.answer: Because it determines the shape and maximum zeroes of the graph.

questions.question: Why is 0 considered a constant polynomial?
questions.answer: Because it has no variable term.

questions.question: Why can a polynomial not have fractional powers?
questions.answer: Because polynomial exponents must be whole numbers.

questions.question: Why is the graph of a quadratic polynomial curved?
questions.answer: Because it involves a squared term.

questions.question: Why is x = 2 a zero of x² − 4?
questions.answer: Because substituting x = 2 gives value zero.

questions.question: Why does x² + 1 have no real zeroes?
questions.answer: Because its graph does not intersect the x-axis.

questions.question: Why is the constant term important in a polynomial?
questions.answer: Because it determines the value of the polynomial at x = 0.

questions.question: Why does the graph of x³ pass through the origin?
questions.answer: Because p(0) = 0 for x³.

questions.question: Why are polynomials useful in real-life modelling?
questions.answer: Because they represent smooth and continuous relationships.
